events.title: United 2026 Bid Formally Announced
events.detail: The United States Soccer Federation, Canada Soccer, and the Mexican Football Federation jointly announced the "United 2026" bid for the FIFA World Cup, marking the first tri-nation hosting proposal in the tournament's history. The bid positioned the joint hosting model as a logistical and commercial breakthrough.

events.title: FIFA Awards 2026 Hosting Rights to United Bid
events.detail: At the 68th FIFA Congress in Moscow, the joint USA/Canada/Mexico bid won the right to host the 2026 FIFA World Cup, defeating Morocco 134 votes to 65. The decision followed controversy over FIFA's anti-corruption framework and concerns about the Moroccan bid's readiness.

events.title: FIFA Confirms 48-Team Expanded Format
events.detail: The FIFA Council unanimously approved the expansion of the World Cup from 32 to 48 teams beginning with the 2026 edition. The new format introduces 12 groups of four teams, with the top two from each group plus the eight best third-place finishers advancing to a Round of 32. The change adds 40 matches and extends the tournament by 7 days.

events.title: FIFA Announces All 16 Official Host Cities
events.detail: FIFA officially confirmed all 16 host cities for the 2026 World Cup: 11 in the United States (Atlanta, Boston, Dallas, Houston, Kansas City, Los Angeles, Miami, New York/New Jersey, Philadelphia, San Francisco Bay Area, Seattle), 3 in Mexico (Guadalajara, Mexico City, Monterrey), and 2 in Canada (Toronto, Vancouver). The announcement confirmed MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, NJ as the Final venue.

events.title: Official Tournament Brand Identity Unveiled
events.detail: FIFA unveiled the official visual identity, emblem, and branding for the 2026 World Cup. The emblem features a stylized trophy design incorporating North American cultural elements representing all three host nations. The identity was revealed in a global ceremony broadcast from Los Angeles.

events.title: Official Match Schedule Framework Released
events.detail: FIFA released the framework match schedule for the 2026 World Cup, confirming tournament dates, venue rotation plans, and the structure of travel clusters. The US will host 78 of 104 matches; Mexico and Canada will each host 13. All quarterfinals, semifinals, and the Final will be played at US venues.

events.title: MetLife Stadium Confirmed as World Cup Final Venue
events.detail: FIFA officially confirmed MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey — the New York Giants and Jets NFL home — as the venue for the 2026 World Cup Final on July 19, 2026. With a capacity of 82,500, it will be the largest venue used in a World Cup since Brazil's Maracanã in 2014. AT&T Stadium (Dallas) and Mercedes-Benz Stadium (Atlanta) were named semi-final venues.

events.title: SoFi Stadium (LA) Confirmed for US Opening Match
events.detail: SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, California — the home of the LA Rams and Chargers — was confirmed as the venue for the United States' opening group stage match on June 12, 2026. The $5.5 billion stadium, opened in 2020, is also slated to host the Opening and Closing Ceremonies of the 2028 Los Angeles Olympic Games.

events.title: Estadio Azteca Set for World Cup Opening Match
events.detail: FIFA confirmed Estadio Azteca in Mexico City as the venue for the tournament's opening match on June 11, 2026 — giving Mexico the honor of kickstarting the first 48-team World Cup. The historic stadium, which previously hosted 1970 and 1986 World Cup finals, underwent renovations to meet modern FIFA standards and safety requirements.

events.title: CONCACAF League Qualifying Phase Begins
events.detail: The CONCACAF qualifying process for non-host nations kicked off with the league phase. CONCACAF allocated 6 berths for the region (beyond the automatic host spots for USA, Mexico, and Canada), with additional intercontinental playoff paths available. The expanded format created high stakes across the Caribbean and Central American nations.

events.title: Argentina Clinches CONMEBOL Top Spot with Record Points
events.detail: Defending World Cup champions Argentina topped the CONMEBOL qualifying table with a record 38 points, confirming their place at the 2026 tournament. Brazil, Colombia, Uruguay, Ecuador, and Paraguay also secured automatic qualification. Bolivia qualified for the intercontinental playoff as the seventh-place team.

events.title: AFC Sends 8 Teams: Japan, South Korea, Iran, Australia, Jordan, Uzbekistan, Saudi Arabia, Qatar
events.detail: Asia's qualifying process concluded with eight direct qualifiers: Japan, Iran, South Korea, Jordan, Australia, and Uzbekistan from the third round, plus Saudi Arabia and Qatar through the fourth round. Iraq advanced to the intercontinental playoff as Asia's remaining contender. All four major Asian football powers earned direct berths.

events.title: Scotland Qualifies for First World Cup Since 1998
events.detail: Scotland secured qualification for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, ending a 28-year absence from the tournament since France 1998. The achievement was celebrated as a watershed moment for Scottish football, capping a significant improvement under national team management and qualifying through the UEFA zone.

events.title: Nine African Nations Qualify: CAF Allocation Expanded
events.detail: Africa's expanded allocation of 9+1 spots was filled, with Algeria, Cape Verde, Egypt, Ghana, Ivory Coast, Morocco, Senegal, South Africa, and Tunisia qualifying directly. Cape Verde made history as a first-time qualifier. DR Congo earned an intercontinental playoff spot by winning a decisive CAF playoff tournament ahead of Nigeria, Gabon, and Cameroon.

events.title: Haiti, Panama, Curaçao Qualify; Curaçao Makes History
events.detail: CONCACAF's three direct qualifying spots (beyond the hosts) were claimed by Haiti, Panama, and Curaçao. Curaçao's qualification was historic — the island nation became the smallest country ever to qualify for a FIFA World Cup by population. Jamaica and Suriname advanced to the intercontinental playoff.

events.title: UEFA Direct Qualifiers Confirmed: England, France, Germany, Spain Lead 12-Team Bloc
events.detail: Twelve European nations secured direct World Cup qualification: England, France, Germany, Spain, Portugal, Netherlands, Belgium, Switzerland, Austria, Norway, Croatia, and Scotland. Four additional European berths remained to be decided by playoff matches in March 2026. Italy — notably — failed to qualify automatically and faced a playoff.

events.title: New Zealand Qualifies as OFC's Direct Representative
events.detail: New Zealand secured Oceania's direct World Cup berth, qualifying for their sixth World Cup. New Caledonia also advanced from OFC to participate in the intercontinental playoff, giving Oceania a chance for a second representative at the 2026 tournament.

events.title: FIFA Relocates Final Draw from Las Vegas to Washington D.C.
events.detail: FIFA moved the Final Draw venue from Las Vegas (the originally planned location) to the Kennedy Center in Washington, D.C., reportedly at the request of President Donald Trump. The change drew criticism from observers who argued it gave the US President an undue platform at a sporting event. FIFA officials cited the prestige of the Washington venue.

events.title: Final Draw Held at Kennedy Center — 12 Groups Revealed
events.detail: FIFA held the official Final Draw at the Kennedy Center in Washington, D.C., with Presidents Trump, Sheinbaum, and Canadian PM Carney opening the first ballot. All 48 teams were drawn into 12 groups (A–L). Key matchups included Mexico vs. South Africa (Group A, opening match), USA vs. Paraguay and Australia (Group D), and England vs. Croatia (Group L). France (Group I) and Argentina (Group J) were drawn into opposite pathways.

events.title: FIFA Awards Trump Inaugural 'Peace Prize' at Draw Ceremony
events.detail: At the Final Draw, FIFA President Gianni Infantino presented Donald Trump with FIFA's inaugural 'Peace Prize,' described as recognition for his 'exceptional and extraordinary actions to promote peace and unity around the world.' The award was created without formal FIFA Council approval and drew immediate criticism from human rights organizations, journalists, and football governance experts who questioned FIFA's independence.

events.title: Full 104-Match Schedule Published
events.detail: One day after the Final Draw, FIFA released the complete 104-match schedule for all venues and dates. The opening match was confirmed as Mexico vs. South Africa at Estadio Azteca on June 11. The US opens June 12 at SoFi Stadium vs. Paraguay; Canada opens June 12 at BMO Field. The Final is set for MetLife Stadium on July 19, 2026.

events.title: Amnesty and HRW Issue Pre-Tournament Human Rights Warnings
events.detail: Amnesty International and Human Rights Watch published coordinated reports warning FIFA and host governments about human rights risks at the 2026 World Cup. Key concerns included US immigration enforcement near venues, LGBTQ+ safety in restrictive states, workers' rights under anti-union federal policies, and press freedom risks. Forty-six civil society organizations signed an open letter demanding action before kickoff.

events.title: Intercontinental Playoff Bracket Set: Guadalajara and Monterrey Host
events.detail: FIFA confirmed the intercontinental playoff bracket and venues for March 2026. Six teams — Jamaica, New Caledonia, Suriname, Bolivia, DR Congo, and Iraq — compete in two three-team mini-tournaments hosted in Guadalajara and Monterrey. The top two teams (one from each bracket) earn the final two World Cup berths. Semifinals play March 26 and finals March 31.

events.title: UEFA Playoff Paths Confirmed: Italy, Wales, Ukraine, Poland Among Contenders
events.detail: The four remaining European World Cup spots were set to be decided in March 2026 playoffs. Path A: Italy vs. Northern Ireland (semi), Wales vs. Bosnia-Herzegovina (semi), with the winners meeting in the final. Path B: Ukraine vs. Sweden (semi), Poland vs. Albania (semi). Italy — three-time World Cup champions — faced the prospect of a third consecutive playoff elimination.

events.title: Sport & Rights Alliance Issues Comprehensive Pre-Tournament Warning
events.detail: The Sport & Rights Alliance — comprising Amnesty International, Human Rights Watch, the ACLU, and Transparency International — published a comprehensive report warning that the 2026 World Cup faces an 'unprecedented convergence of human rights challenges.' The report detailed concerns over US anti-union federal policies exposing workers to exploitation, corruption risks in host nations, press freedom restrictions, and FIFA's inadequate enforcement of its own 2016 Human Rights Policy.

events.title: Intercontinental Playoff Semifinals: Four Nations Compete for Two Spots
events.detail: The intercontinental playoff semifinals took place in Guadalajara and Monterrey on March 26, 2026. New Caledonia faced Jamaica (Guadalajara) and Bolivia faced Suriname (Monterrey), with the winners advancing to the finals on March 31. The tournament determines which two confederations — OFC/CONCACAF and CONMEBOL/CONCACAF — earn the final World Cup berths.
